name: Apple Inc.
assessment: Apple's April pullback aligns with broader large-cap tech de-rating rather than company-specific deterioration. Quality scores remain in the top decile and free cash flow conversion is intact. The MOMENTUM score has compressed from 7.8 to 6.4 over the period, consistent with the regime rotation rather than fundamental erosion. Services growth at 14% YoY continues to offset hardware cyclicality. Risk: China iPhone demand remains the swing factor for the next two quarters.
rationale: Score-vs-price divergence indicates the drawdown is regime-driven; fundamentals stable.
